title: lotus subpinnatus var. wrangelianus
intro: Lotus subpinnatus var. wrangelianus, also known as Amur lotus or lotus root, is a type of aquatic plant that belongs to the Nelumbo genus, characterized by its long-stemmed, pinnate leaves and showy flowers. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine, where it is believed to have anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Lotus subpinnatus var. wrangelianus include its potential to lower blood sugar levels, improve cardiovascular health, and support digestive health, among others. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and rhizomes are often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
